response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned Staff-level engineer with 15+ years of software development experience and current work at Google on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) frameworks, which aligns directly with the role's agentic workflow focus. However, the role demands 8+ years specifically building production-grade AI solutions, and his substantive GenAI work appears limited to his current Google role started in April 2025. Critical gaps include no Python in his skills or history (the role's primary language), no mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph or CrewAI, and no evident forward-deployed or customer-facing consulting experience.
strengths: Current Staff-level role at Google directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, mapping precisely onto the role's agentic workflow and MCP server requirements
Extensive TypeScript production experience across multiple companies, plus hands-on AWS CDK at Meta, covering two of the role's key required technologies
Demonstrated technical leadership and mentoring through Refactoring Dojos, Selenium course delivery, and engineering craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume skills list and employment history, yet it is listed as a primary required language alongside TypeScript
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, or equivalents), which are core to the role
The 8-year minimum for production-grade AI solutions is not satisfied — his AI and GenAI work is essentially limited to his current position, which began in April 2025

key_factors.factor: Current MCP and agentic infrastructure work at Google
Absent Python and RAG/vector DB skills
